title: aster alpinus subsp. subvillosus
intro: Aster alpinus subsp. subvillosus, also known as the alpine aster, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the daisy family, characterized by its hairy leaves and purple flowers. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which have been attributed to its high content of flavonoids and saponins. The health benefits of Aster alpinus subsp. subvillosus include reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and alleviating respiratory problems, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to support overall well-being and boost the immune system.
